behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes. A pure unit test of file-filter logic can miss real failures in wiring between PR diff ingestion, path normalization, and scan orchestration. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network is strictly required if using local fixture diffs and filesystem. MOCK-HIDE: yes, over-mocking changed-file inputs can hide inclusion/exclusion bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the analysis pipeline end-to-end across modules using a fixture PR diff plus a repo fixture containing changed and unchanged files; assert only changed-file paths are passed to rule execution.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: moderate. Unit tests of formatter alone can miss schema mismatches between linter output, grouping logic, and comment payload builder. REAL-INFRA: no real network/API needed to catch grouping failures. MOCK-HIDE: yes, if comment posting is fully mocked without validating composed payload.
behaviors.test_description: Feed mixed-severity findings through real aggregation + comment-render modules and assert the final comment body contains correctly partitioned error/warning/info sections with expected entries.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: yes. This is a runtime/performance boundary tied to process execution, I/O, and CI-like resource constraints. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: high risk; mocked timing or mocked linter execution would likely miss true timeout regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real lint step as a subprocess against a synthetic 200-file fixture repo in CI-like runner limits, and assert wall-clock completion remains under 10 minutes with artifacts/comments generated.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: yes. Correctness depends on handling an actual subprocess crash/exit behavior, not just in-process exceptions. MOCK-HIDE: yes, mocked linter calls often cannot reproduce signal/exit-code and partial-output edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline with the real linter subprocess on a fixture set containing one crash-inducing malformed file plus valid files; assert the job continues, analyzes remaining files, and emits/postes consolidated results including crash reporting.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: low. The failure boundary is usually deterministic dedup logic (same file/line/rule fingerprint merge). REAL-INFRA: no. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function/object with overlapping findings for identical file+line (including different rule IDs where policy requires collapse) and assert exactly one output entry per dedup key.
